description: Manifest of publishable Feather DB content. The Feather website's Claude-code agent fetches this manifest first, then pulls individual markdown files. Update this file whenever a new article/asset becomes publish-ready.

blog_posts.title: You don't need GPT-4o full-context for AI memory — Feather DB beats it for $2.40
blog_posts.summary: Feather DB v0.8.0 + GPT-4o scores 0.693 on LongMemEval_S, beating the LongMemEval paper's full-context GPT-4o ceiling (0.640). Cheap-tier with Gemini-Flash hits 0.657 for $2.40 per benchmark run.
blog_posts.headline_metric: 0.693 on LongMemEval_S with GPT-4o · 0.657 with Gemini-Flash
blog_posts.cover_image_spec: Bar chart: Feather (both tiers) vs full-context GPT-4o ceiling vs naive RAG. White bg, blue/orange. Inter font. No competitor names.
do_not_mention: Zep head-to-head numbers in launch creative

marketing_assets.title: Feather DB GTM Plan — v0.8.0 LongMemEval Launch
marketing_assets.summary: Positioning, ICP, channel plan, KPIs, asset checklist, founder talking points. Treat as source-of-truth for the launch.

technical_reports.title: Feather DB on LongMemEval — Full Report
technical_reports.summary: Full benchmark report with all caveats, competitor comparison (including Supermemory), reproducibility commands, per-axis breakdowns. The internal/honest version — distinct from the public blog post which omits competitor head-to-heads.

papers.title: Feather DB: An Embedded Vector Database with Adaptive Temporal Decay and Hybrid BM25/Dense Retrieval
papers.version_changes: Added §4.7 LongMemEval empirical results (0.693 with GPT-4o, 0.657 with Gemini-Flash).

raw_data.title: Per-run benchmark result JSONs
raw_data.purpose: Audit trail. Every benchmark number cited anywhere in our content links back to a JSON in this directory.

rules_for_consumers: Always preserve front-matter at the top of markdown files when re-rendering.
Never edit the raw markdown — fetch fresh, render fresh.
If a content item's status is not 'ready', do not auto-publish it.
If a content item lists 'do_not_mention' entries, those phrases must NOT appear in the rendered output.
When publishing, use the canonical 'always_include_links' for any cross-references; do not invent URLs.
Cite headline_metrics by the values in this manifest, not from the markdown body (the manifest is the single source of truth).
On any content update detected (commit SHA changed for the file), re-publish to all listed channels.

watcher_recipe.description: Suggested approach for the consumer agent (e.g. Feather website's Claude code) to detect changes and re-publish
steps: 2. Compare last-seen file SHAs (use https://api.github.com/repos/feather-store/feather/contents/<path>) to detect changes.
3. For changed files with status='ready', fetch the markdown via raw_url.
5. Log a successful publish event back to a state file.
